commit.message:
feat: unify model lists, add workspace overrides to runner type gates (#794)

## Summary

Eliminates duplicate model lists and restores feature-flag gating for
models. `models.json` is now the **single source of truth** for all
models across all providers — no hardcoded fallback lists anywhere. Adds
workspace-scoped override support to runner type feature gates and
cross-provider model/runner validation.

**Problem:** PR #788 added hardcoded model lists to the agent registry.
The create-session dialog read models from the registry instead of the
`/api/models` endpoint, bypassing all Unleash feature flag and workspace
override logic. The `featureGate` field on runner types only checked
global Unleash — no workspace-level control. Nothing prevented using a
Gemini model with a Claude runner.

**Solution:** Models belong in `models.json`, not the agent registry.
Each runner now has a `provider` field, and the models API filters by
provider. Model/runner provider mismatches are rejected at the API
boundary — including for default models.

## What changed

### Backend

- **`handlers/models.go`** — Added `?provider=` query param filter.
`isModelAvailable` validates model/runner provider match
(`requiredProvider` param) for all models including defaults. Returns
503 when manifest unavailable (no hardcoded fallbacks). Returns empty
`defaultModel` when no models pass filtering.
- **`handlers/runner_types.go`** — Removed
`Models`/`DefaultModel`/`ModelOption`, added `Provider`. New
`GetRunnerTypesGlobal` (admin). Updated `GetRunnerTypes` to
project-scoped with workspace overrides via
`isRunnerEnabledWithOverrides`.
- **`handlers/sessions.go`** — Resolves runner provider from registry,
passes to `isModelAvailable`. Degrades gracefully when registry
unavailable (skips provider matching, still validates against manifest).
- **`cmd/sync_flags.go`** — `FlagsFromManifest` skips provider default
models (from `providerDefaults` map) in addition to the global default.

### Operator

- **`internal/handlers/registry.go`** — Synced `AgentRuntimeSpec`:
removed `DefaultModel`/`Models`/`ModelEntry`, added `Provider`.

### Manifests

- **`agent-registry-configmap.yaml`** — Removed `models`/`defaultModel`,
added `provider` field per runner.
- **`models.json`** — Added Gemini models. Added `providerDefaults` for
per-provider default model resolution.

### Frontend

- **`create-session-dialog.tsx`** — Models fetched from API filtered by
`selectedRunner?.provider`. No hardcoded fallback lists. Static
`DEFAULT_MODEL` for form init, `useEffect` updates from API.
- **`services/api/runner-types.ts`** — Removed
`models`/`defaultModel`/`RunnerModel`/`requiredSecretKeys`. Added
`provider`. Two API functions: project-scoped + global.
- **`services/queries/use-models.ts`** — Provider in query key with
conditional spread (no `undefined`).
- **`app/api/projects/[name]/runner-types/route.ts`** — New proxy route.
- All consumers updated to pass `projectName` or use global variant.

## Data flow

```
User selects runner type (e.g., "Gemini CLI", provider="google")
  → useModels(projectName, open, "google")
    → GET /api/projects/:name/models?provider=google
      → Backend filters models.json by provider
      → Checks Unleash flags + workspace overrides
      → Returns only enabled Google models (or empty defaultModel if none)

User creates session with model
  → Backend resolves runner provider from registry
  → isModelAvailable checks (in order):
    1. Model exists in manifest and is available
    2. Model provider matches runner provider (rejects cross-provider)
    3. Default models skip flag check but still enforce provider match
    4. Non-default models pass feature flag check (workspace override → Unleash)
  → Mismatches rejected with 400

Manifest unavailable (cold start)
  → Try ConfigMap volume mount
  → Try in-memory cache
  → 503 Service Unavailable (no hardcoded fallbacks)
```

## Tests

| Test | Coverage |
|------|----------|
| Provider filtering (3 tests) | `?provider=anthropic`,
`?provider=google`, no filter |
| Provider mismatch (3 tests) | Wrong provider → false, matching
provider → true, provider-default with wrong provider → false |
| `isRunnerEnabledWithOverrides` (4 tests) | Override true/false, no
override, nil overrides |
| `GetRunnerTypesGlobal` (2 tests) | Returns ungated runners, no auth
required |
| `FlagsFromManifest` (2 tests) | Skips global default, provider
defaults, unavailable |
| 503 on manifest unavailable (2 tests) | Missing file, malformed JSON |
| `isModelAvailable` (10 tests) | Empty, default, available,
unavailable, unknown, fail-open, workspace overrides, provider
match/mismatch |

## Checklist

- [x] `gofmt` clean, `go vet` clean, `golangci-lint` 0 issues
- [x] `npm run build` — 0 errors, 0 warnings
- [x] `eslint src/ --quiet` — 0 errors
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass
- [x] All new tests pass (1 pre-existing session test failure on main)
- [ ] Select Claude runner → see only Anthropic models
- [ ] Select Gemini runner → see only Google models
- [ ] Toggle model flag in Feature Flags admin → model list updates
- [ ] Enable `runner.gemini-cli.enabled` workspace override → Gemini
runner appears
- [ ] POST session with mismatched model/runner → 400 rejection
- [ ] POST session with provider-default model on wrong runner → 400
rejection

Add AI chat interface with Claude tool-use for recipe management (#16)

* Add Phase 3 AI Agent Chat implementation plan

7-step plan for building the chat interface: system prompt & tool
definitions, SSE streaming with agentic tool loop, Fastify route
handler, React chat widget (floating panel + mobile sheet), and
root layout wiring. Designed for parallel execution across API
and UI agents.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* Add comprehensive test plan for Phase 3 AI chat

Adds Step 7 (Tests) covering:
- API: tool executor unit tests (mocked DB), agentic stream loop
  tests (mocked Anthropic SDK), Fastify route integration tests
- UI: use-chat hook tests (mocked fetch/SSE), chat-messages render
  tests (empty state, tool calls, recipe links, auto-scroll),
  chat-input interaction tests (Enter/Shift+Enter, disabled states)

Bumps lint/build verification to Step 8.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* Add CRUD mutation tools and comprehensive test coverage to Phase 3 plan

Expands tool set from 4 read-only tools to 18 tools:
- Read: search_recipes, get_recipe_details, get_inventory,
  find_recipes_by_ingredients, get_grocery_lists, get_grocery_list_details
- Recipe mutations: create_recipe, update_recipe, delete_recipe
- Inventory mutations: add/remove_inventory_item,
  add/update/remove_inventory_component
- Grocery list mutations: generate/save/update/delete_grocery_list

Adds mutation SSE event type for UI cache invalidation + toast
notifications. Updates system prompt to include mutation safety rules
(fetch-before-modify, confirm destructive actions). Adds detailed
test cases for all 18 tool executors, mutation event emission in
stream tests, and cache invalidation + toast tests in UI hook tests.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* feat: implement Phase 3 AI agent chat

API backend (api/src/chat/):
- system-prompt.ts: Cooking assistant persona with mutation safety
  rules, data schema reference, and today's date for expiration logic
- tools.ts: 18 tools (6 read + 3 recipe + 5 inventory + 4 grocery
  mutations) with Anthropic Tool[] definitions and executeTool()
  dispatcher. All mutations return isMutation flag + mutationDetail
  for SSE events. Uses existing DB patterns, FTS/embedding sync,
  audit logging with "chat." prefix
- stream.ts: Agentic loop using client.messages.create() with
  claude-haiku-4-5. Processes text/tool_use blocks, emits SSE events
  (text/tool_start/tool_end/mutation/done/error), caps at 15 tool
  rounds, accumulates token usage
- handler.ts: POST /api/chat Fastify route with Better Auth session
  validation, Zod body validation (1-50 messages, max 10000 chars),
  rate limit 10/min, SSE response streaming
- Extract shared Anthropic client to lib/anthropic-client.ts

UI frontend (ui/src/components/chat/):
- use-chat.ts: Custom hook managing ephemeral chat state, SSE stream
  parsing with chunk buffering, React Query cache invalidation on
  mutation events, AbortController lifecycle
- chat-messages.tsx: Message display with suggested prompts (empty
  state), user/assistant message bubbles, tool call spinners with
  context-aware labels, regex-based markdown rendering (bold, italic,
  links via TanStack Router, lists), auto-scroll
- chat-input.tsx: Textarea with Enter-to-send/Shift+Enter-newline,
  send/stop button toggle, auto-resize (1-4 rows)
- chat-widget.tsx: Floating action button → fixed panel (400x600px,
  responsive), auth-gated, header with clear/close

Tests (64 API + 31 UI = 95 new tests):
- tools.test.ts: 41 tests covering all 18 tool executors with mocked
  DB, FTS, embeddings, audit
- stream.test.ts: 12 tests for agentic loop, mutation events, abort,
  error handling, max rounds, token accumulation
- handler.test.ts: 11 tests for auth, validation, SSE headers
- use-chat.test.ts: 11 tests for hook state, SSE parsing, cache
  invalidation, abort
- chat-messages.test.tsx: 10 tests for prompts, rendering, markdown
- chat-input.test.tsx: 10 tests for keyboard, disabled states

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* fix: use destructured useSession pattern in ChatWidget

Match the codebase convention of `const { data: session, isPending } =
useSession()` instead of accessing `session.data` directly. Also guard
on `isPending` to avoid flash-of-null during session loading.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* fix: split assistant messages after tool calls to avoid duplication

When the agentic loop runs multiple rounds (text → tools → text),
all text was concatenated into a single assistant message, causing
the "thinking" preamble and final answer to appear together as
duplicated content.

Now resets assistantId after tool calls complete so the next text
event creates a new message bubble. Gives a natural flow:
  1. Assistant: "I'll check your pantry..."
  2. [Searching recipes... ✓]
  3. Assistant: "Here are your top matches: ..."

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* fix: suppress intermediate text from tool rounds to eliminate duplication

Two changes to fix repeated message content:

1. stream.ts: Only emit text blocks from the final round (stop_reason
   !== "tool_use"). Intermediate text where Claude narrates "I'll check
   your pantry..." before calling tools is suppressed — the UI already
   shows tool_start/tool_end progress indicators.

2. system-prompt.ts: Instruct Claude to not narrate tool usage ("Let me
   search..." etc.) since the UI handles progress display.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* fix: only emit text from tool-free final round to prevent duplication

The previous approach checked stop_reason === "tool_use" to suppress
text, but Claude can produce text+tools across multiple rounds where
each round's text restates the answer. Now checks whether each response
actually contains any tool_use blocks — if it does, ALL text from that
round is discarded. Only the final round (with zero tool_use blocks)
emits text to the client.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* Fix runtime error: remove leftover pendingText reference

The pendingText variable declaration was removed in a previous refactor
but an assignment on line 106 was left behind, causing a ReferenceError
when the chat agent enters a tool-use round.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* Fix double messages: move fetch out of React state updater

doStream() was called inside setMessages() updater, which React
StrictMode double-invokes — causing two parallel API requests and
duplicate assistant responses. Use a ref to read current messages
and call doStream outside the updater.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* Emit text from tool-use rounds to fix empty chat bubbles

Previously text was suppressed during tool-use rounds to avoid
duplication, but now that the double-fetch bug is fixed this just
causes empty assistant bubbles. Emit roundText in all rounds.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* Filter out empty assistant bubbles from chat messages

Skip rendering assistant messages that have no text content and no
active (in-progress) tool calls. These empty messages are left behind
when tool-use rounds complete and a new message is created for the
final text response.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* Fix indentation from prettier formatting

https://claude.ai/code/session_01P7FdDYiUGV3SiMLjo5XEWr

* fix: double messages

* fix: ui warnings

* fix: throw TRPCError when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing

getAnthropicClient() was throwing a plain Error instead of a TRPCError,
causing the CI test assertion to fail. Updated to throw TRPCError with
INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR code and fixed the test message assertion to match.

feat: redesign Spark Dockerfile with Isaac Sim 5.1.0 + IsaacLab 2.3.0 + VNC

Isaac Sim 5.1.0 officially supports DGX Spark (ARM64 aarch64).
Redesign Spark image as a near-mirror of the x86_64 workstation.

Spark Dockerfile changes:
- Two-stage build (builder + groot) mirroring workstation
- CUDA 13.0 base (GB10 GPU requires CUDA >= 13)
- Miniforge (ARM64-native conda) with Python 3.11
- PyTorch 2.9.0+cu130 (aarch64 wheels)
- Isaac Sim 5.1.0 via pip (multi-arch)
- IsaacLab v2.3.0 (DGX Spark support)
- Full VNC desktop: TurboVNC + XFCE4 + Chromium + desktop shortcuts
- CycloneDDS + unitree_sdk2_python (robot communication)
- unitree_sim_isaaclab + unitree_rl_lab with task patches

commit.message:
fix: reduce stale listing index propagation + notify on soft delete (#483)

* fix: prevent coordinator reconnects from GetExternalIP spam and spurious netlink events

Two root causes of coordinator mesh reconnects in Docker-compose environments:

1. GetExternalIP() was called on every 10s heartbeat. In Docker containers
   (private IPs only), this triggered HTTP requests to ifconfig.me/ipify on
   every heartbeat. Intermittent failures caused the returned IP to oscillate,
   triggering IPsChanged() → DisconnectAll().

   Fix: add a permanent in-process cache (extIPCache/extIPValid). The result
   (even "") is cached until ResetExternalIPCache() is called explicitly from
   HandleNetworkChange, ensuring a single fetch per genuine network event.

2. shouldIgnore() on Linux and Darwin passed events with Interface="" through
   to HandleNetworkChange. On Linux, IPv6 RTM_NEWADDR events lack IFA_LABEL
   so ifName stays ""; on Darwin, InterfaceByIndex() failures produce the same
   result. These unidentifiable events triggered spurious DisconnectAll().

   Fix: change shouldIgnore to return true for empty Interface on Linux and
   Darwin. Add an IfAddrmsg.Index fallback in the Linux netlink parser so real
   IPv6 address changes on identifiable interfaces still pass through correctly.
   Windows is intentionally unchanged (its callback structurally has no iface).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prune replicated state vectors and drop empty bucket index entries

Two remaining memory-leak fixes after #475:

1. repl_queue.go: PruneDeletedKeys now uses allKeys (local + replicated)
   instead of localKeys when building the activeSet. Previously, state
   vectors for replicated objects were never eligible for pruning because
   localKeys excluded peer-owned buckets. After deletion on the primary,
   a replica could accumulate N accordion-run worth of stale vectors
   (~9-12 MB) before they aged out. Now deleted replicated objects are
   pruned after GCGracePeriod (10 min) just like local ones.

2. admin_s3_listing.go: updateListingIndex("remove") now deletes the
   bucket key from the map when both Objects and Recycled are empty after
   the removal, rather than leaving a zero-byte entry that reconcileLocalIndex
   would clean up 5 min later. Negligible individual impact (~36 bytes/bucket)
   but keeps the index map tidy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: promote per-call GC http.Client to Server struct singletons (#482)

Two admin handlers (forwardGCToPeers, forwardBucketDeletionToPeers)
created a fresh http.Client + http.Transport on every invocation,
causing a new TLS handshake to every peer coordinator on each GC run.
Promote these to gcForwardClient and shareGCClient fields initialised
once in NewServer(), sharing a single Transport so connections are
kept alive across calls.  Both clients use InsecureSkipVerify (mesh
uses self-signed certs) with MaxIdleConnsPerHost=4 / IdleConnTimeout=90s
to match the existing s3ForwardTransport pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use mesh CA for GC client TLS instead of InsecureSkipVerify

The gcForwardClient and shareGCClient were initialized with
InsecureSkipVerify: true. The mesh already has a proper internal CA
(set via SetMeshTLS on startup). Promote gcTransport to a Server field
(gcMeshTransport) and hook it into SetMeshTLS() so it receives the same
mesh CA TLS config as s3ForwardTransport and meshTransport.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reduce stale listing index propagation + notify on soft delete

Primary: after persisting the local listing index to the system store,
immediately enqueue it for replication. This shrinks the stale-data window
from ~5 minutes (next auto-sync cycle) to ~50 seconds (replication
propagation time). In accordion mode each iteration generates unique
filenames, so stale coord-2/3 listing files would accumulate as "thousands
of phantom files" for up to 5 minutes after cleanup.

Secondary: wire a new SetOnObjectRecycledCallback into Store.RecycleObject
so that soft-deleted objects are immediately moved from the live objects
list to the recycled list in the listing index. Previously, soft-deleted
objects remained visible as "live" until PurgeObject fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v10.3: Unleash capital deployment — remove bottlenecks throttling trade activity

Pipeline audit found 82% trade size reduction: AI recommends $100, bot executes $18.
Root cause: Kelly ceiling + pattern confidence + cooldown cascade + re-entry bar.

Changes:
- Kelly ceiling: 8% → 12% for portfolios under $10K (dynamic getEffectiveKellyCeiling)
- Pattern confidence floor: 0.2 → 0.5 (max 50% reduction, was 80%+)
- Re-entry confluence: +40 → +32 BUY, -35 → -30 SELL (20% easier re-entry)
- Cooldowns: near-continuous (HOLD 30s, WEAK_SIGNAL 15s, TRADE 2min)
- Hard lock: 3min → 2min (stop-losses handle risk, not cooldowns)

Net effect: AI $100 → Kelly $60 → confidence $30-60 (was $7-18).
Risk unchanged — ATR stops, sector limits, circuit breaker still fully active.

commit.message:
Fix VLC Lua extension so the dialog actually opens and works

Core bugs fixed:
- descriptor was a local variable, not a function - VLC couldn't read it
- vlc.input.add_callback() does not exist in VLC Lua API, crashed on load
- add_slider() had wrong argument order, causing layout crash
- zoom_slider/txt:add_callback() not valid VLC dialog API, crashing dialog
- zoom passed as string to vlc.var.set, must be a number

Rewritten with correct VLC extension structure:
- function descriptor() returns metadata table
- function activate() / deactivate() / close() / input_changed()
- clean 4-column dialog layout with status label, crop coords, zoom, buttons
- refresh_dimensions() auto-fills Width/Height from actual playing video
- do_apply() builds VLC crop geometry string (WxH+X+Y) and applies instantly
- do_reset() clears crop/zoom and restores input fields

Rewrite WalletService for Privy SDK + cross-platform key derivation

WalletService now has production-ready architecture for Privy integration:

- initializePrivy() called at app launch (restores session for returning users)
- connect() handles both new users (create embedded wallet) and existing
  web users (Privy automatically recovers same wallet = same address)
- Key derivation uses PBKDF2 via CommonCrypto with parameters that EXACTLY
  match the web's walletEncryption.ts:
    Message: "Amach Health - Derive Encryption Key\n\n...\nNonce: {address}"
    Input:   signature hex bytes (strip 0x, decode hex)
    Salt:    wallet address hex bytes (strip 0x, first 40 chars = 20 bytes)
    Hash:    SHA-256
    Rounds:  100,000
    Output:  256-bit key → lowercase hex string
- Fixed critical incompatibility: old iOS stub used SHA256(signature_utf8)
  which would produce different keys than the web's PBKDF2 derivation
- #if canImport(PrivySDK) guards allow building without the SDK installed
  (falls back to dev-mock mode with real PBKDF2 pipeline exercised)
- Keychain storage unchanged (functional, properly secured)
- Added hasAuthenticatedBefore flag (UserDefaults) for onboarding flow

ROADMAP.md added with deferred items:
  P2: Unified Storj memory schema (cross-platform sync)
  P4: Session summarization via LLM
  P5: True SSE streaming
  Plus web onboarding steps to port later

Next: Add Privy SDK via Xcode (File → Add Package Dependencies →
github.com/privy-io/privy-ios), set App ID + Client ID in WalletService.

commit.message:
Fix mesh_run stuck: reduce defaults and cap cross-context growth

- Default max_rounds: 3→2 (adversarial), 4→3 (full), 1 (council)
- Default call_timeout: 120s→60s per provider call
- Cap cross-context per message to 1500 chars to prevent quadratic
  prompt growth across rounds (was unbounded)

Before: 3 rounds × 2 models × 120s timeout × growing prompts = 5+ min
After:  2 rounds × 2 models × 60s timeout × capped prompts = ~30-60s
